title: Colon Cancer's Special Germs
summary: Scientists found something special about colon cancer. It has its own unique germs, called a microbial fingerprint. This is different from other cancers. The study looked at over 9,000 cancer patients. They used DNA from tumors to see what germs were inside. Only colon cancer had these special germs. This discovery could help doctors treat colon cancer better. They might use it to diagnose the disease more accurately. The research was done by Dr. Abraham Gihawi and his team. They hope this will improve cancer care. Colon cancer is a common and serious disease. Understanding its germs could lead to new treatments.

keywords.explanation: a type of cancer in the large intestine
the material that carries genetic information
to find out what disease someone has

questions.question: What did scientists find special about colon cancer?
How many cancer patients were in the study?
What could this discovery help doctors do?
What did scientists use to study the germs?

background_read: Colon cancer is a disease where cells in the large intestine grow out of control. It is common and can be deadly. Germs, or microbes, are tiny living things like bacteria. Scientists study germs to understand diseases better. DNA is like a code that tells our bodies how to work. In this study, scientists looked at DNA from cancer tumors to find special germs only in colon cancer. This could help doctors in the future.
Article_Structure: WHO: Dr. Abraham Gihawi and his team from the University of East Anglia
WHAT: Found that colon cancer has a unique microbial fingerprint
WHERE: In the UK, using data from cancer patients
WHY: To help doctors diagnose and treat colon cancer better

perspectives.description: The study shows colon cancer has special germs, which could lead to new ways to fight the disease.
